INTRODUCTION
Since Peter Senge is a follower of ‘Systems Thinking’, he states that
because people work in tandem with things, the ecosystems survive. He
acknowledges that since people have been working together, communities
have survived and if they wouldn’t have, communities would have
disappeared. By developing ‘learning organization’ concept for a
workforce, Peter Senge utilizes the method of systems thinking to facilitate
the companies to have more success. In a learning organization, a
community of the total workforce is created and the team members learn
together and share their opinions. This creates thinking that is
interconnected bringing them on a similar wavelength. It promotes more
openness, productivity and communication through shared and organized
learning resulting in more progress of the team.
 
The book presents the thoughts and ideas of Senge in a concise manner. It
states the basic five disciplines that are essential for the members of a
learning organization and each of these disciplines are equally important. It
promulgates the notion that in case of team learning, a greater synergized
knowledge is created as compared to individual and disconnected learning
since it aims at developing shared perspectives and eliminates egos. It
enables business organizations to have a broader perspective and resolve
long-term issues rather than focusing on short-term solutions
 
The Fifth Discipline, since its first publication in the year 1990, has been an
inspiration to several organizations that have recognized worth of the ideas
of Senge. The concept is over 20 years old but is still discussed in the world
of business. Managers whose businesses have faced problems due to
learning paralysis as well as repetitive mistakes have sought inspiration and
guidance from The Fifth Discipline. The book has been used by the
companies to increase the speed of growth at both business and personal
level. The book has achieved the status of a handbook for leaders and the
second edition of the book published in 2006 exemplifies the growing
importance and popularity in a dynamic business environment.
OceanofPDF.com


MAIN IDEA
Since business evolves consistently, it is imperative that we continue
learning to have competitive edge. Those organizations that emphasize on
learning stay ahead of competition and are more successful. For
implementing new techniques, ideas and methods it is important to identify
the mistakes that we are making.
 
Learning should not only be limited as a personal practice but we should
rather promote it within our own organizations while not just encouraging it
but rather enhancing it. The learning organizations must be used as
platforms to solve bigger problems through encouragement of communal
values which refer to a united workforce with similar visions and through
evaluating the structures that underlay and support attainment of results that
we seek.
 
It is clearer now that those businesses where learning is one of the core
functions of their workforce are more successful. Businesses are constituted
of individuals who form a collective. The aggregate of the collective’s
knowledge and experience enable organizations to achieve greater success.
Businesses that are able to maximize and optimize the talents and abilities
of the individuals while facilitating their assimilation into a single collective
are more successful.
OceanofPDF.com


BOOK SECTION OVERVIEW
SECTION ONE
The Competitive Advantages of Learning
The world of business has cut-throat competition and to survive, it is
important that organizations learn to be flexible, must be adaptive and
should possess a greater level of ingenuity. Learning organizations nurture
flexibility, ingenuity, ability to innovate and think ahead and also the ability
to adapt to newer systems. Although this concept sounds simpler that it is, a
remarkably large number of organizations fail in making optimum use of
the learning practices. Very often, businesses attempt to thrive only on basis
of their intuition. However, experience and knowledge form the foundation
for intuition and higher the level of experience and knowledge, better is the
intuitive ability for solving problems.
SECTION TWO
The 
Five 
Core 
Disciplines 
of 
Learning
Organizations
Mastering the five basic disciplines is important for learning organizations:
1. Personal Mastery
Ability to master the energy, focus and patience of oneself results in a well-
rounded person who is of great significance for an organization.
2. Mental Models
Our decision making is influenced by our understanding of our prejudiced
perceptions and ingrained mentality.
3. Building Shared Visions


A vision shared by the team regarding the future of the company is more
beneficial as compared to disconnected visions promulgated by employees
who are obsessed with themselves.
4. Team Learning
A powerful and fulfilling collective is created by the combined expertise
and knowledge brought together by team work.
5. Systems Thinking
Systems thinking’ enables companies to focus on the larger picture and
provides them with long-term solutions that are more sustainable than short-
term solutions to the inherent problems.
 
Although it is possible that these disciplines can be learned individually, it
is more beneficial to see them as a whole. For instance, Systems thinking,
the last of the five disciplines is fulcrum and moving forward without it is
similar to starting the car that has wrong engine.
OceanofPDF.com


SECTION THREE
The Key Challenges, Practical Problems and
Issues Learning Organizations Face
 
Consider that a learning organization is an experiment and follows a process
based on trial and error. When problems are presented, new lessons are
learnt while trying to overcome them. Facing problems is inevitable for a
business and learning organizations help prepare for the problems by
practically testing them even before they occur.
 
General problems that a company faces includes:
 
Internal politics
Exclusive power
Insufficient time for learning
Inability to maintain a decent work and life balance
Repeated mistakes
Difficulties with leading and organization that is a learning
organization
 
Overcoming these issues is critical for an organization. Still unbelievably
several companies are yet to identify feasible solutions for these problems
and many companies have completely failed to address these issues. The
very basic nature of the learning organization is to present these issues
thereby facilitating the finding of a solution. Learning organization supports
the admission of the fact that problems do exist. Problems may not be
clearly visible for a while but ultimately they will surface and cause
damage. A learning organization’s job is to ensure that we are prepared
when the problems surface.
OceanofPDF.com


BOOK SECTION DETAILS
SECTION ONE
THE 
COMPETITIVE 
ADVANTAGES 
OF
LEARNING
MAIN IDEA
Businesses are affected by the external changes. When, outside of a
company parameters change, the effects of such changes can cascade into a
disaster if the organization is unable to positively respond to changes. The
problems may even worsen if the organizations are not able to understand
what changes are taking place around them. The ability for adapting to
critical changes and responding to them is nurtured by the learning
organization. Above all, it teaches the requirement for being prepared when
the disaster strikes.
SUPPORTING IDEAS
Due to its ability to learn, a learning organization is a step ahead as
compared to other organizations. Several organizations fall short for the
following reasons:
 
Employees become stagnant. They are scared of change since their positive
has become their identity. A learning organization promoted versatility and
helps identify employees by determining their well-rounded abilities and
the number of functions that they are capable of performing.
It may be termed as loyalty or conceit, but generally whenever a company
faces a tumultuous situation, it is attributed to the competition instead of the
own performance of the company. This is however misguided and not
conducive for change. Bad habits are a result of our failure to acknowledge
the mistakes made by us.
 
Generally businesses react to consequences when an event occurs, for
example reduced revenue instead of rooting out the primary cause of the


situation. It is important to look at larger picture. By focusing on the effect,
its reoccurrence is being encouraged. For instance, a doctor who diagnoses
a patient with headache will try to identify the root cause rather than giving
a few pills to the patient and sending them away.
 
Due to their ingrained habits, a non-learning organization is unable to see
the larger picture. It is like putting the blame on the goalkeeper of the soccer
team when a goal is conceded instead of identifying the root causes of the
problems that resulted in the goal, like a poor defense. A non-learning
organization constantly reacts to consequences of the events and makes
themselves handicap. A learning organization identifies the root cause of the
problems and presents the solution that eliminates the possibility of the
reoccurrence of the problem.
 
Learning organizations evaluate the behavioral patterns that might result in
the arising of the issue. By doing this, they alter the patters and effectively
control their own destinies. Alternately, non-learning organizations continue
being reactive instead of being proactive and simply put off the things that
would continue to come back to haunt them.
 
A non-learning organization is passive.
A learning organization is active.
 
More importantly, learning organizations are:
1. Forward Thinking
Mistakes are eliminated by continuous learning.
2. Dynamic
Shared learning and team-work are emphasized.
3. Productive
Since the entire team continues to learn, each of the members is able to
optimize the strengths of each other and this leads to higher production
4. Communal
Production and shared knowledge are the key elements. Sharing of talents
and continuous communication take the teams forward.
5. Innovative – A learning organization leads by introducing improvements
that are genuinely effective.


Quotes - Building Learning Organizations
“Building learning organizations involves developing people who learn to
see as systems thinkers see, who develop their personal mastery and who
learn how to surface and restructure mental models collaboratively. Given
the influence of organizations in today’s world, this may be one of the most
powerful steps towards helping us ‘rewrite the code’, altering not just what
we think, but our predominant ways of thinking. In this sense, learning
organizations may be a tool not just for the evolution of organizations, but
for the evolution of intelligence.” - Peter Senge
 
“Learning disabilities are tragic in children, but they are fatal in
organizations. Because of them, few corporations live even half as long as a
person - most die before they reach the age of forty.” - Peter Senge
OceanofPDF.com


SECTION TWO
THE 
FIVE 
CORE 
DISCIPLINES 
OF
LEARNING ORGANIZATIONS
MAIN IDEA
The Core Five Disciplines of a Learning Organization:
1. Personal Mastery
Ability to master the energy, focus and patience of oneself results in a well-
rounded person who is of great significance for an organization.
2. Mental Models
Our decision making is influenced by our understanding of our prejudiced
perceptions and ingrained mentality.
3. Building Shared Visions
A vision shared by the team regarding the future of the company is more
beneficial as compared to disconnected visions promulgated by employees
who are obsessed with themselves.
4. Team Learning
A powerful and fulfilling collective is created by the combined expertise
and knowledge brought together by team work
5. Systems Thinking
Systems thinking’ enables companies to focus on the larger picture and
provides them with long-term solutions that are more sustainable than short-
term solutions to the inherent problems.
SUPPORTING IDEAS
1. Personal Mastery
Ability to master the energy, focus and patience of oneself results in a well-
rounded person who is of great significance for an organization.
 
An organization by itself is intangible and inanimate and exists via the
activities and consciousness of the individual members. Therefore,
organizations themselves do not learn; rather the individuals who constitute
organizations must learn.


Master Personal Mastery:
The problem solving and intellectual growth of individuals are promoted. It
facilitates in maturing of a person to make them a well-rounded individual
who is capable of bringing greater resolve, intuition, foresight and
discipline into their respective organizations.
 
It helps promoting new skills.
It enables individuals to improve themselves, their peers and
organizations.
It leads to a journey that helps achieve various experiences full of
advantages.
It embeds within individuals the desire for continuously gathering
knowledge and improving oneself in the various facets of life.
 
These improvements positively impact their professional lives.
 
Learning organizations are the total and combined knowledge possessed by
the individual members. Businesses that promulgate a large volume of
learning from each of the members become significantly strong. If
knowledge can be considered as power, the combined knowledge of all the
members can be considered as mega-power.
 
There is no end to personal mastery and therefore an individual is
constantly urged to learn and by this they are able to achieve greater of the
following:
 
Vision
A person with high level of personal mastery has goals that are clearer and
they are capable of envisioning their future more clearly.
 
Realism
Everyone dreams and some of the dreams are more realistic as compared to
others. There is higher chance of these dreams becoming reality by personal
mastery. Individuals are taught to be more realistic while also being aided in
attainment of their objectives.
 
Power


Few things are beyond our control and these cannot be changed. However,
when it comes to business, everything can be changed. When you start
identifying the underlying reasons, you achieve more power. Identifying
causes and not just their effects result in empowerment. We are better able
to spot errors with personal mastery.
 
Subconscious
Without sounding Freudian, most of us depend upon our subconscious for
resolution of problems. Most of the information that we receive gets stored
in the subconscious and though we believe that we have lost significant
parts of it over the years, it often surprises us when those memories
resurface suddenly whenever we face a problem. It is referred to as
intuition. Therefore, out intuition develops more with learning.
 
The basic flaw that a non-learning organization has is that it does not
support the thought that there can be individual personalities that is bold
enough to say what is on their mind and challenge status quo. In such
organizations, everyone is merely considered as a part of the machinery and
they are expected to continue working without questioning anything. A
learning organization promotes personality, individuality and also personal
mastery. Such organizations promote honest, frank and open work
environment that nurtures unique perspectives and encourages innovation.
Quotes - Personal Mastery
“The core leadership strategy is simple; be a model. Commit yourself to
your own personal mastery. Talking about personal mastery may open
people’s minds somewhat, but actions always speak louder than words.
There’s nothing more powerful you can do to encourage others in their
quest for personal mastery than to be serious in your own quest.” - Peter
Senge
2. Mental Models
Our decision making is influenced by our understanding of our prejudiced
perceptions and ingrained mentality.
 


A major problem with regards to business is the fact we try to perceive its
working in the mind and this can stall progress. For instance, while reading
a poem, we visualize it in a certain way; however when we read something
that is totally different, we discard it for not being a poem, although it could
be a totally innovative type of poem.
 
We have a prejudiced perception when we determine the way an
organization must function and it is based on our previous experiences.
Altering the existing mindset of an organization must be one of the basic
goals and important targets of the learning organizations. Changing the
mindsets must precede changing of reality. When you are able to enter
someone else’s mind, you are better placed to influence the decision making
of theirs. However, due to the inherent prejudices, several businesses
repeatedly make the same mistakes due to their stubbornness.
Master Mental Models
Exists in mind
Resist change
Affect actions
 
Create alternatives to change the mental models
 
A mental model that is cemented does not change easily provided there are
no stronger and more feasible alternatives. If someone can be convinced to
examine a particular situation in a different manner, they might be
motivated to change their mindset.
 
Create new power in the boardroom.
We look towards a ‘higher power’ to get inspired and for mental models and
knowledge. We try to mimic those from whom we are trying to learn. If a
new board of directors with new ideas is appointed, it will motivate the
team to reevaluate their existing ideas and preconceptions.
 
Create self-awareness.
Just a few of us know that we possess mental models. Motivating people to
become calculative and considerate and to analyze and reflect upon their


thoughts as to why do they think what they think, they become more aware
of the mental models of theirs that exist and this allows them to reform.
 
Get people to ask questions.
Interactions with others help unearth the mental models that exist and
questioning can reveal how the mental models influence our behavior.
 
Characteristics of learning organizations facilitate becoming aware of
our own mental models. This comes as a challenge to us for replacing and
improving them and to utilize these models to benefit our organizations. A
large number of organizations restrict themselves to barely a few of the
mental models; the ones that are at disposal of the employees of the
company. Raising an awareness of these helps organizations to develop
completely new models that are of advantage to them. Likewise, once a
newer mental model is activated in individuals, they are soon replaced by
even new mental models. A learning organization facilitates moving ahead
and experimenting. They discourage stagnation.
3. Building Shared Visions
A vision shared by the team regarding the future of the company is more
beneficial as compared to disconnected visions promulgated by employees
who are obsessed with themselves.
 
Several among us have visions. Learning organizations aim at getting the
collective of people to share visions that are common. When all of us are
committed towards working for common goals, the result could be
outstanding. However, if the visions are disjointed, it will result in conflicts
and subsequently to failure. Shared visions are nurtured by each team
member and it is worked upon and harnessed by each of the insightful and
powerful individuals.
Master Building Shared Visions
Suppressing egos
Whenever there are ego conflicts among individuals, there is no possibility
of having a shared vision. Shared visions are not created by competing but
rather the idea of everyone must be give due credence and should be heard.


Each of these ideas however must contribute for developing a
comprehensive shared vision among the team.
 
Encourage individuals for sharing their visions
When a larger number of people share their visions, it increases their feeling
of worthiness and self-esteem. It fully utilizes their personal strengths and
also increases the strength of the entire collective. If individuals feel that
they have been left out, it would lead to resentment.
 
Shared vision should be given space to breathe
It is important to allow shared visions to life their own lives. They must be
allowed to gradually grow on their own rather than being influenced by any
specific individual. If there are any disagreements, they must be resolved. It
will result in improving the life of shared visions.
 
Govern
Although shared vision requires freedom to grow, it is crucial that it is
directed in the right manner to ensure that the momentum is not lost. It can
be done by asking questions such as. ‘What is being created?’, ‘what is the
ideology?’ and ‘is the vision aligned with the actual ideals?
 
Keep things in check
Although the vision must be allowed to flourish, it must also be kept in
check to comply with reality. An unrealistic vision is just overselling
oneself.
 
It is proven by experience that a learning organization is better for
development of a shared vision as compared to a non-learning organization.
Shared visions are more potent and powerful vis-à-vis personal visions and
are made stronger by the synergy of several great minds. It is similar to a
skyscraper which grows till it towers over the city and becomes the
centerpiece. The shared vision of the learning organizations is similar to the
skyscraper that becomes the city’s centerpiece, the sum total of the
individual expressions.
Quotes – Shared Vision


“When you are immersed in a vision, you know what needs to be done. But
you often don’t know how to do it. You run an experiment because you
think it’s going to get you there. It doesn’t work. New input. New data. You
change direction and run another experiment. Everything is an experiment
but there is no ambiguity at all. It’s perfectly clear why you are doing it.
People aren’t saying, ‘Give me a guarantee it will work.’ Everybody knows
there is no guarantee. But the people are committed nonetheless.” - Ed
Simon, CEO, Herman Miller
4. Team Learning
A powerful and fulfilling collective is created by the combined expertise
and knowledge brought together by team work.
 
There is limitation to what an individual is capable of achieving, especially
in a competitive business world. When a synergy of individual learning is
created, the combined output is extraordinary. Team learning results in
combination and collaboration for pointing the company along with all the
assembled and acquired skills towards a particularly clear direction.
Consequently, the organization is able to attain all goals much faster.
Team learning process seeks:
Sharing skills
Coordination for action
Developing and nurturing interaction
Defining one single and clear goal
Promoting and refining communication
Taking the business to next level
Master Team Learning:
Creating platforms for an open debate
Learning increases when topics are discussed among the people. When they
debate on a point along with their peers, their arguments become more
informed and rounded. This exposes the weaknesses and strengths of all
those involved which trims off the fat.
 


Encouraging conflict
Conflict exists at the core of a debate. When people argue, their different
perspectives and arguments result in flow of more knowledge, information
and leads to enlightenment. Instead of playing down conflict while
discussing, it is feasible to embrace it
 
Creation of learning platforms.
To enhance team learning, one of the good methods is taking the employees
off their desks and to an environment that encourages them to learn, bond
and discuss.
 
Team learning is much better than individual learning. Teams that learn
together, move forward better and ultimately win as a team. When persons
learn as individuals, they form different techniques, ways and ideas as
compared to their peers. These differences in vision and style result in slow
performance and chaos. Everyone must have the same wavelength and
learning as a team promotes shared visions, goals and results in efficiently
creating workplace harmony.
5. Systems Thinking
Systems thinking’ enables companies to focus on the larger picture and
provides them with long-term solutions that are more sustainable than short-
term solutions to the inherent problems.
 
It is to be noted that the title of the book is The Fifth Discipline. The last
and fifth discipline is Systems Thinking and is the fulcrum. The first 4
disciplines will fail without this discipline. These five principles are
complementary and are individual parts of a whole. They are like a car that
will struggle to efficiently perform efficiently if a major component of the
car is missing. Systems Thinking is like the engine of the car and it acts as
the force that drives the performance.
 
We are encouraged by systems thinking to look at the entire picture instead
of few snapshots. It promotes people to identify patterns that affect their
performance and helps them analyze these factors to stem the


improvements. Systems thinking does not merely identify an event’s
consequence as a quick fix but rather tries to eliminate the cause.
Master Systems Thinking:
Focuses on avoiding long-term problems. Simply focusing on short-term
problems and their issues will result in a continuous barrage of them.
 
The main reason for the poor performance of a company is its system.
Therefore, just like you will not push your own body beyond limits for
winning a race while also knowing that something is stopping you, you
must look carefully for the reasons that result in poor performance of your
business. If anything physical holds you back while running on the race
track, you must seek the advice for your system if it is slow.
 
Quick fixes are discouraged by the systems thinking. If you believe that a
problem can completely be rooted out just by finding a makeshift and
temporary solution, you cannot be farther from truth. It is as pointless as
using glass wall for stopping an avalanche.
 
Shortcuts to finding solutions are discouraged by Systems thinking. Shabby
work is a symbol of a shabbily organized company and if one cannot work
towards analyzing their systems, it reflects poorly upon the entire business.
 
It is important to focus on both, the cause and its effect. Several
organizations solely focus on effects and ignore causes. It is important to
address the causes and the causes of those causes.
 
To boost the system, even a small amount of challenge is sufficient. For
instance, mechanics can easily fix cars because they know what needs to be
done and a little tweak can also fix the car. Very often the basic underlying
issue in a system is very simple and is easily visible. A learning
organization helps in looking at such issues in a more clear and quick
manner.
 
Experience is rewarded by the systems thinking. With more analysis of the
system, more quickly the work can be completed and the success rate will


be higher.
 
Organizations are large entities and similar to a human body, they are
constituted of a large network of organs, veins and arteries that are linked to
each other. Organizations have a large network of elements that
communicate to one another and work together. Hence, while examining the
problem, one must not focus only on one part of the organization but
instead must focus on the entire organization as well as the other parts.
 
The purpose of systems thinking process is not to hamper relationships.
Pointing fingers on others and blaming them will not contribute positively
towards the larger picture. Therefore, blame game must be avoided since it
will only hamper the morale of the others and will divert focus from the
main issues.
 
Evaluating Cause and Effect
Cause explains the ‘why’ factor; the reason for the occurrence of the issue.
Examining it helps with explaining why something has gone wrong. You
will not be able to provide a long-term and viable solution unless all the
factors that led to the issue are considered. Failure to do so will only help
find temporary and partial solutions that have negative impact and the issue
will persist.
 
Analyzing the cause enables identifying its root cause. It is possible to
destroy Japanese Knotweed if it is uprooted and similarly, a problem can be
eliminated by uprooting its cause. This enables you to reach the core of the
issue.
Analyzing the root cause of an issue is helpful in actually identifying it. It
facilitates not only in eliminating the specific issue, but will also allow you
to refine the process for a long time.
Similar to Japanese Knotweed, which is an invasive plant and grows in an
unrestricted manner until it is stopped, a problem also functions in a
similarly invasive manner. It can lead to:
 
Unstoppably growth
Adversely affect the growth of the business
 


The method is simple. It involves finding the underlying issues and
eradicating them and this will lead to the growth of the business.
 
An understanding of the benefits of causes and effects allows looking
beyond mere symptoms of the issue and identifying the actual causes of the
symptoms. Doctors simply do not extract the cancerous mole from a
patient; they try to attack the cause of it. Similarly, you must attempt to go
deeper into your system’s jungle.
 
Leverage
Systems thinking helps create leverage. It is possible due to focused,
sustained and an analytical and critical thinking to eradicate underlying
issues that adversely affects the performance of the business. Generating
leverage is not easy since it includes a considerable amount of risk since
both losses and profits are magnified to a large extent. However, systems
thinking helps create the probability of leverage by enabling businesses
with producing better results that are more positive. When the structure that
exists behind a result is considered, it is possible to create the leverage.
 
Looking at ‘The Whole Picture’
If a builders’ team is unable to consider a house’s structure that is being
built by them, it will be an unpardonable mistake. Despite this, several
businesses ignore the very structure responsible for their results. This is
harmful and detrimental to the business and can eventually result in the loss
of the revenue.
 
A look at the whole picture enables companies to identify the areas where
the business is going wrong. They are helped to identify the underlying
issues and also other elements that affect the performance of their business.
This enables them to optimize the way they work and it will lead to
improved results going forward. The emphasis is on the longer term rather
than short term.
 
Those companies that continue with focusing with small pictures are
working in a blind manner. Such working style leads to poor habits and
ignorance and also creates a negative impact over their employees and
peers. Systems thinking focuses on opening the eyes.


Quotes - Systems Thinking
“The eye not only has to see; it has to think.”- Paul Cezanne
 
“Ultimately, mastering the language of systems thinking also requires the
other complementary learning disciplines. Each contributes important
principles and tools that make individuals, teams and organizations more
able to make the shift from a linear perspective to seeing and acting
systematically.” Peter Senge
OceanofPDF.com


SECTION THREE
THE KEY CHALLENGES, PRACTICAL PROBLEMS AND ISSUES LEARNING
ORGANIZATIONS FACE
MAIN IDEA
Consider that learning organizations are a base for trial and error methods
that allow confronting the problems and facilitate attempts for resolving
them. They perform the role of solutions providers.
CRUCIAL ISSUES
Internal politics
Problems due to ‘Exclusive Power’
Insufficient time for learning
Inability to find a proper work-life balance
Recurring mistakes
Difficulties faced while leading a learning organization
 
Overcoming these factors is important for a company. However, several
businesses still have not found feasible solutions for these problems and
few of them even fail to address these issues. Learning organizations due to
their basic nature deliberately evaluate these issues so that their solutions
finding process can begin. Learning organizations make us realize that there
are problems and even if they are not visible yet, they will eventually
surface and learning organizations help us to prepare for that situation.
 
1. Internal Politics
In a business environment, ego clashes can be high and when people work
on a vision that is shared by all, such ego clashes can be detrimental as one
person would keep trying to push their vision. Successful organizations are
those organizations where a successful idea emerges due to team efforts and
not due to individual efforts and the emphasis on knowing who propounded
the original idea does not exist.
 
Remember:


Organization is bigger than everyone
An organization’s will comes before anyone else’s will
The mindset of individuals must be challenged
People must learn forging common values instead of disjointed values
that lead to conflicts.
 
Organizations can be severely affected due to internal politics and the
primary objective of a company must be to work together since disharmony
among the employees and their ego clashes will lead to disaster. Individuals
are better motivated and work efficiently in an environment that is
encouraging and allows for clear communication and debates and
promotion of voices of individuals. In the instances when the voices are
suppressed, ideas are not heard and this leads to narrow mindedness which
is detrimental for the company.
 
2. Problems due to ‘Exclusive Power’
Assigning responsibilities help a person feel worthy. Large number of
responsibilities results in better learning and more work. Additionally if
people are allowed to make decisions, they feel more motivated to work.
Delegation of responsibilities enable employees to feel more worthy and
this improves their abilities. Organizations that gives responsibilities to
every individual and allow them to make decisions reap higher rewards
since it inspired them to work harder.
 
In a learning organization, there is a focus on localizing the power.
Organizations must be careful while assigning responsibilities and
delegating powers to them by determining their extent. Managers must
continue to exercise control in spite of following a democratic system in the
workplace.
 
3. Insufficient time for learning
Business consumes a large amount of time and several of the businessmen
have very little time for socializing and have to quite often compromise and
make sacrifices to continue being successful. Since the time is limited, it is
pertinent to ask, ‘how would such employers manage to find time for
creating learning organizations?’ and ‘how will the employees be able to get
the time for learning?’


 
Though it may sound ruthless, it is imperative that businesses have to make
the time if they want to learn. It is more cost efficient to learn, especially in
the longer run than to work with ignorance and to nurture habits that are bad
and not conducive.
 
Managers who use learning organizations can afford to claim to be
progressive, innovative and forward thinking. They will be able to
successfully eliminate those bad habits which were previously responsible
for long and short term issues. Managers that fail to find time to learn will
be continuously affected by the same problems repeatedly.
 
The success of a business depends not on what happened earlier but what
would happen in future. Adopting the learning organizations allows
managers to be prepared for the future and to control the destiny of their
company.
 
4. Inability to find a proper work-life balance
Finding the right work and life balance is difficult for businessmen who
have to work hard and for longer hours; resulting in them having no social
time. However, a learning organization helps individuals, through personal
mastery to not just have a better productivity but also to have a better life
outside of their work. It facilitates individuals to be better not just in their
work life but also personal life and they can reap the benefits by having a
happy and stable workforce. Learning organizations discourage any conflict
between social and work life.
 
Therefore:
Individuals are encouraged to prioritize based on what is more important for
them and what place does a thing have in their life’s hierarchy.
 
It encourages spending time with the family and it helps improve all the
aspects of the life and these learnings can be used to have a better life at
home.
 
Individuals are encouraged to open up about their personal problems to
their organization since family and social issues affect the work


performance. Honest and frank discussion about the life’s issues with peers
in the organization helps with resolution of the conflicts.
 
It must be the responsibility of the organizations to help individuals to
improve the balance between their social and work life.
 
5. Recurring mistakes
Everyone makes mistakes, but successful businesses learn from these
mistakes. By knowing the cause of the mistake helps avoid them in the
future. Learning organizations use the power of Microworld for better
accumulation of experiences and it helps eradicate niggling mistakes.
 
Microworld helps analyze the way we do things and this helps determine
how efficient our methods are and if they need replacing.
 
Microworld offers solutions for short term in a kind of ‘slow motion’ that
can be analyzed for us to examine if such a quick fix is effective or not.
Similarly, the long term events are presented as active agencies that
demonstrate their beginning and the end results for helping us to determine
if they are potent enough.
 
Microworld helps bring all the business activities under a single system that
is unified.
Microworld enables experimenting with our actions. By doing it, we are
better placed to test existing as well as new processes and contrast and
compare their efficiency.
It encourages critical thinking and analysis of data. It gives managers
sufficient time for reflecting.
 
Due to the nature of Microworld as an experimentation laboratory,
inventiveness and creativity are encouraged. Daring and risky ideas are
encouraged and it allows them to be tested.
 
Consider Microworld to be the stage when a movie is edited. It allows
addition of things, taking them away and experimenting with them and
enables testing them without any specific consequences. Microworld gives


you sufficient time for testing theories before they are applied to the real life
situations.
 
6. Difficulties faced while leading a learning organization
Strong and unique personalities are the traits of leaders. Their charisma,
authority and the ability for thinking quickly define them as leaders.
 
The following qualities must be possessed by the leaders in thinking
organization:
 
Openness
Foresight
Communication skills
Creativity
 
Leaders in learning organizations are a guide and friend and have the role of
a teacher for those who work with them. They are entrusted with the
responsibilities for utilizing the strengths and abilities of the employees and
direct the organization for achieving clear objectives, shared vision and
common purpose. Leaders must lead as being an example for others to
follow and use the above specified 5 disciplines.
Quotes - Section Three
“Systems thinking, personal mastery, mental models, building shared vision
and team learning - these might just as well be called the leadership
disciplines as the learning disciplines. Those who excel in these areas will
be the natural leaders of learning organizations. Ultimately, people follow
people who believe in something and have the abilities to achieve results in
the service of those beliefs. Or, to put it another way, who are the natural
leaders of learning organizations? They are the leaders.” - Peter Senge
OceanofPDF.com


CONCLUSION
The Fifth Discipline as a book has rich content that explains the learning
organizations’ concepts in a deft and simple manner. The manner in which
managers must implement their framework and ideas is explained clearly. It
acts as a handbook or a guide or even like Bible that aims at eradicating
mediocrity and leading the way towards change and progress. The five key
disciplines are combined into a single cohesive unit that forms the
foundation of the future success of a company and helps create a highly
efficient synergy capable of replacing dull business functioning, visions that
are short sighted and eliminates mistakes that are repetitive. The book
grants access to an exciting, vibrant and new world of business.
 
Learning organizations’ concept is a major breakthrough in the world of
business in the last several decades and Peter Senge is the right man for
envisaging the concept as well as outlining it. In a composed, cool and a
highly absorbing manner, Senge reveals the structural composition of
learning organizations and their basic fundamentals and ethos. He is able to
do all of it with ease and empowers learners, leaders and the disciples of the
concept of systems thinking. Managers who embrace the message given by
Senge will do themselves a big favor.
